candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue line on the income statement; best match for total revenue/sales.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit revenue line as the top-line measure.
No clear cost of goods sold/cost of sales line is present. This appears to be a financial/insurance-style statement where direct production/service COGS is not separately reported.

candidates.why: Explicit administrative overhead label; although preview is empty, row presence indicates reported data in at least one year and it is the closest explicit SG&A-style row.
Clear operating overhead component, typically part of SG&A/administrative expenses.
Clear administrative overhead component and part of SG&A.
Operating overhead bucket; useful coverage because the explicit administrative row appears mostly empty in preview.
Contains administration wording but it is on the balance sheet, so not appropriate for income-statement SG&A mapping.

variables.notes: Applied SG&A hierarchy rule (b): an explicit SG&A-style row exists ('Administrative Expense') but appears mostly empty in the preview, so included it and added overhead buckets/components to improve coverage. Excluded depreciation/amortization, impairment, interest, taxes, and totals.
No dedicated research and development expense line is present above or below operating profit.

final_choice.row_label: Net finance expenses from insurance contracts

variables.notes: The candidate list clearly misses the best main interest-expense label: 'Interest and other financing expense', which appears on the income statement and is the preferred reported financing cost line. Chose it from the full preview and set manual review accordingly. Added candidate-listed fallback lines as lower-priority alternatives. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Interest and other financing expense

candidates.why: Best match for book equity attributable to shareholders, and it excludes non-controlling interest because 'Equity' equals this line plus the separate NCI line.
Total equity includes non-controlling interest here, so less preferred for BE due to double-counting risk with MIB.
Only one equity component, not total book equity.

candidates.why: Asset line, not shareholders' equity.
Income statement investment income concept, not equity on the balance sheet.
Income statement line, not book equity.

variables.notes: Preferred shareholder/owner equity line to avoid double counting with separately reported non-controlling interest.

Income statement allocation of comprehensive income, not balance-sheet minority equity.
